mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding output, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is always present as a trend-following indicator.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of INDPRO level on 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the Sharpe ratio of long signals is not positive over the full backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6%, it signals sustained labor market strength, leading to higher consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If, after UNRATE dips below 6%, the subsequent 60-day forward return is not positive in at least 55% of instances over the 2005-2014 period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in both housing starts and retail sales reflects robust domestic demand, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon. The joint signal is more predictive than either series alone.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce an average 20-day forward return significantly different from zero (t-test p>0.10) over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term in a regression model is not statistically significant (p<0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a zero lower bound environment, any increase in the federal funds rate above 0.25% signals a tightening stance that reduces monetary accommodation, contracting equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If, when FEDFUNDS > 0.25% and the ZLB regime is active, the average 20-day forward return is not negative (or is positive with t-test p<0.05), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payroll employment signals a healthy labor market that supports consumer spending and economic expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days without any threshold conditions.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year Rank IC of PAYEMS month-over-month change on 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 or the mean return of positive payroll months is not positive over the sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI year-over-year inflation rate exceeds 2.5%, it triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If, when CPI YoY > 2.5%, the average 60-day forward return is not negative (or positive with p<0.05) over the 2005-2014 period, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volumes indicate immediate consumer demand strength, which is quickly priced into equities, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as a high-frequency trend signal.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return following positive month-over-month RSAFS changes is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During the early recovery phase from a recession, simultaneous increases in housing starts and industrial production indicate strong rebound momentum, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The joint signal is regime-specific.
failure_condition: If, in periods identified as recovery (based on NBER business cycle dating), the joint signal does not produce average 20-day returns >0 with Sharpe >0.5 over 3-year windows, the hypothesis is rejected.
